---
title: "modelling clutch size"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(performance)
library(MASS)
library(pscl)
library(dplyr)
library(glmmTMB)
```

AMBIENT, WARM AND HOT LARVAL TREATMENT
```{r}
results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#make maternal ID a factor
results$maternalID <- as.factor(results$maternalID)

#how many eggs were counted in total
sum(results$clutch_size) 

#mean and SD of clutch size, including clutches of 0 eggs
mean(results$clutch_size)
sd(results$clutch_size)

#make a treatment combination column
results <- results %>%
  mutate(treatment_combination = paste(larval_treatment, pupal_treatment, sep = "_"))  %>%
  mutate()

#number of mated females in each treatment group 
table(results$treatment_combination)


results_filtered <- results %>%
  filter(
    larval_treatment %in% c("ambient", "warm", "hot")) %>%
  filter(clutch_size != 0) %>%
  filter(!is.na(egg_mean_1A)) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(weight_mg, egg_mean_1A, clutch_size),
    ~ as.numeric(scale(.)),
    .names = "{.col}_z"
  ))

results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "warm","hot")),
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "cool","mean","warm","hot"))
  )
```

```{r}

#including ambient pupal treatment
results_filtered %>%
  group_by(larval_treatment) %>%
  summarise(mean_clutch_size = mean(clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            n = n(),
            sd = sd(clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE))



#not including ambient pupal treatment (same as used in PSEM)
results_filtered %>%
  filter(pupal_treatment != "ambient") %>%
  group_by(larval_treatment) %>%
  summarise(mean_clutch_size = mean(clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            n = n(),
            sd = sd(clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE))

```

```{r}
#look at the distribution of clutch size
ggplot(results, aes(x = clutch_size)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of clutch size",
       x = "clutch size",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()


#look at the distribution of female mass
ggplot(results, aes(x = weight)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.005) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of female mass",
       x = "female mass",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

#look at the distribution of mean egg sizes
ggplot(results, aes(x = egg_mean_1A)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.01) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of mean egg size",
       x = "mean egg size",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}
lm1 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment * pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), family = nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
summary(lm1)

#as expected from path analysis, including the ambient group doesn't change anything - female mass is signficant, no direct effect of larval or pupal temp on clutch size

lm1_no_interaction <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment + pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), family = nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
anova(lm1, lm1_no_interaction)
#interaction term doesn't improve fit


lm1_no_larval <- glmmTMB(
  clutch_size ~ pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1 | maternalID),
  family = nbinom2,
  data = results_filtered)
anova(lm1_no_interaction, lm1_no_larval)
#larval treatment doesnt improve fit

lm1_no_pupal <- glmmTMB(
  clutch_size ~ larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1 | maternalID),
  family = nbinom2,
  data = results_filtered)
anova(lm1_no_interaction, lm1_no_pupal)
#pupal treatment doesn't improve fit

lm1_no_weight <- glmmTMB(
  clutch_size ~ larval_treatment + pupal_treatment + (1 | maternalID),
  family = nbinom2,
  data = results_filtered)
anova(lm1_no_interaction, lm1_no_weight)
#weight does improve model fit

icc(lm1)
#grandmother identity explains just 4% of the variation in clutch size

```


COMPARISON BETWEEN WILD AND AMBIENT
```{r}
results_filtered_wild <- results %>%
  filter(larval_treatment %in% c("wild", "ambient")) %>%
  filter(clutch_size != 0) %>%
  filter(!is.na(egg_mean_1A)) %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "wild")))

results_filtered_wild %>%
  filter(pupal_treatment != "ambient") %>%
  group_by(larval_treatment) %>%
  summarise(mean_clutch_size = mean(clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            n = n(),
            sd = sd(clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE))
```

```{r}
lm2 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment, family = nbinom2, data = results_filtered_wild)
summary(lm2)
```
